title: Yakirra australiensis
taxonomy: Yakirra australiensis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It is native to Australia and belongs to the subfamily Faboideae. Yakirra australiensis is further classified into the tribe Phaseoleae and is closely related to other legumes found in Australia. The classification of Yakirra australiensis provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and classification history of Yakirra australiensis.
uses: Yakirra australiensis has been traditionally used by Indigenous Australians for its medicinal properties, although its specific uses may vary across different communities.
